evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: help us move faster, make better decisions, and maintain trust with schools and learners; ship meaningful improvements every month
evidence.domain_risk: Become our day-to-day lead for data privacy and school-facing data assurance - supporting data sharing agreements and DPIAs, and ensuring our practices around children's data are robust and consistently applied
evidence.data_team_maturity: keep our warehouse/dbt/Lightdash stack reliable and scalable - without slowing the business down
evidence.jd_authorship: Embed 'instrumentation before shipping' so every major release launches with the right KPIs, tracking, dashboards, and alerts - enabling fast learning, quick diagnosis, and confident iteration
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Strengthen use of measurement in product processes: Embed 'instrumentation before shipping' so every major release launches with the right KPIs, tracking, dashboards, and alerts - enabling fast learning, quick diagnosis, and confident iteration
evidence.autonomy_level: set data strategy and own Data team OKRs
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Put in place pragmatic governance and monitoring for AI features that touch learner responses and scoring; keep our warehouse/dbt/Lightdash stack reliable and scalable
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Putting new governance and monitoring in place for AI features is new-build work, while keeping the existing warehouse/dbt/Lightdash stack reliable and scalable is fix/scale work, together spanning both modes.
evidence.work_arrangement: Hybrid working: minimum two days in office per week
evidence.ai_role: Put in place pragmatic governance and monitoring for AI features that touch learner responses and scoring
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Candidate builds governance and monitoring infrastructure that AI systems depend on to operate safely.
evidence.testing_framing: Become our day-to-day lead for data privacy and school-facing data assurance
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Candidate owns data quality and assurance practices with direct leadership responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: supporting data sharing agreements and DPIAs, and ensuring our practices around children's data are robust and consistently applied
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Candidate operates in highly regulated domain with children's data requiring compliance frameworks (DPIAs, data agreements).
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple velocity signals ('move faster', 'ship meaningful improvements every month', 'ships quickly') paired with substantial rigour signals ('data governance', 'privacy-by-design', 'DPIAs', 'robust', 'responsible AI').
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Education sector involves sensitive children's data and explicit GDPR/compliance requirements (DPIAs) but lacks financial reporting or banking-grade regulatory consequences.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools and infrastructure (warehouse, dbt, Lightdash) exist and responsibilities emphasize scaling/improving rather than building from zero.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Technically specific with named tools (dbt, Lightdash), precise methodologies (instrumentation before shipping, impact evaluation design), and company context (A-Levels, GCSEs, learner scoring).
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Product analytics and measurement in product development is the first responsibility in the 12-month plan, with explicit emphasis on KPIs, feature impact measurement, and embedding measurement into product processes.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role explicitly owns strategy definition and OKRs for the data function, not execution of someone else's plan, positioning this as a leadership role that shapes direction.
